content:
type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding 'transformers' param to Encoding.encode(text, *, transformers=None, allowed_special=..., disallowed_special=...) — accepts a single callable(str)->str or a list/sequence of such callables, applied sequentially to text before encoding, before the special-token disallow check runs on the transformed text.

fields.note: Adding 'transformers' param to Encoding.encode(text, *, transformers=None, allowed_special=..., disallowed_special=...) — accepts a single callable(str)->str or a list/sequence of such callables, applied sequentially to text before encoding, before the special-token disallow check runs on the transformed text.

content:
type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add an optional 'transformers' keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, transformers=None, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all') -> list[int]. Type: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, normalize it into a list (wrap a single callable in a list; if it's already a list/tuple/sequence of callables, use as-is), then apply each callable in order to , each receiving the previous callable's output (text = fn(text) for fn in transformers_list), BEFORE the existing disallowed_special check and BEFORE calling self._core_bpe.encode. So the disallowed-special-token regex check and the actual BPE encoding must run against the TRANSFORMED text, not the original. Everything else in encode() (allowed_special/disallowed_special resolution, the UnicodeEncodeError fallback via utf-16 surrogatepass, return type list[int]) stays as-is. Do not change the function's return type or add positional args -- 'transformers' should be keyword-only, added anywhere in the keyword-only section (order relative to allowed_special/disallowed_special doesn't matter). No need to touch encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, or encode_batch -- only the main encode() method needs this.
note: encode() is the only place my feature touches; happy to have you own tiktoken/core.py entirely and fold this in alongside your return_positions work since your change is more deeply intertwined with encode's internals. I will not edit tiktoken/core.py.

content:
type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: CORRECTED (previous message had a shell quoting bug, please use this version instead): Add an optional keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, transformers=None, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all') -> list[int]. Type: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, normalize it into a list (wrap a single callable in a list; if already a list/tuple/sequence of callables, use as-is), then apply each callable in order to text, each receiving the previous callable output (i.e. text = fn(text) for fn in transformers_list). This must happen BEFORE the existing disallowed_special regex check and BEFORE calling self._core_bpe.encode(text, allowed_special), so both the disallowed-special check and the actual BPE encoding run against the TRANSFORMED text, not the original. Everything else in encode() stays as-is: allowed_special/disallowed_special resolution logic, the UnicodeEncodeError fallback that re-encodes via utf-16 surrogatepass, and the list[int] return type. transformers should be keyword-only; position among the other keyword-only args does not matter. No changes needed to encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, or encode_batch -- only the main encode() method.
note: encode() is the only place my feature touches; happy to have you own tiktoken/core.py entirely and fold this in alongside your return_positions work. I will not edit tiktoken/core.py.

content:
type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented transformers param myself in my own sandbox copy (our working trees are isolated, your edits weren't visible here), tested and wrote patch.txt. Thanks for the coordination -- FYI for future rounds, cross-agent edits don't share a filesystem in this setup so each agent still needs to write its own complete patch.
